growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.units: qualitative (consumed; routed via Wood-Ljungdahl/reductive acetyl-CoA pathway)

up.method: amino acid LC-MS of spent medium

growth_records.cite: Weiss, Anna S. et al. In vitro interaction network of a synthetic gut bacterial community. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01153-z. [PMC8941000]
growth_records.snippet: fast growing strains (GR > 1.5 h-1, ... B. coccoides YL58) ... Both Lachnospiraceae strains generated high amounts of acetic acid ... Formic acid ... consumed by T. muris YL45 and B. coccoides YL58 ... alanine was especially depleted by B.
growth_records.notes: growth_rate 1.5 h-1 = lower bound of 'fast' bin (exact in Table S2, unavailable). Produces high acetate + lactate; consumes formate (via Wood-Ljungdahl, gapseq-supported) and alanine. All qualitative (MS); no numeric rates. Acidifies medium.

growth_records.agit: 300 rpm (stir bar) for monoculture growth curves; Hungate tubes otherwise
growth_records.cite: Pau Pérez Escriva et al. Distinct N and C Cross-Feeding Networks in a Synthetic Mouse Gut Consortium. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01484-21. [PMC9040589]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Blautia coccoides YL58 (Firmicutes), Max OD 1.73, Mean μ ± SD 0.6 ± 0.07.
growth_records.notes: μ=0.6 h^-1 (±0.07) reported. td = ln2/μ = 0.6931/0.6 = 1.155 h. Max OD600 = 1.73. Xanthine and histidine supplementation improved max OD ~10-20% (Fig 4A, no numeric μ in text). No per-biomass exchange rates in main text.
